notes: Cross-service shared-library culprit with delayed onset. The ai-memory-svc deploy at 17:12 (v2026.06.22-1712) ships commit 7e2c9b41 to the SHARED persistence lib pkg/memstore/dynamo_writer.go, whose diff replaces the BatchWriteItem grouping loop (<=25 items/request) with a per-item PutItem loop. Each memory write now consumes a separate write unit, so consumed_write_capacity climbs ~200/min for ~8min from the 320 baseline, breaching the 2400 provisioned ceiling at ~17:20; dynamodb_write_throttle_ratio then ramps to 0.58 and the table throws ProvisionedThroughputExceededException. WARN lines 'single-item PutItem to memory-store (batching disabled)' precede the ERROR throttles and point straight at the mechanism. ai-memory-svc is first-failing; ai-agent-svc is blast radius (memory persist 503s). Decoys: a1af4c1 is an INNOCENT ai-memory-svc commit (compaction-helper tidy, 17:08) that is the most-recent change ON the failing service and touches its own files but cannot produce write throttling; c036 (platform-api request-id) and ff6268 (web banner) deploy at ~17:19 right at onset; a flag flip memory_compaction_v2 off->on at 17:19:10 is a distractor. Rollback the ai-memory-svc deploy to restore BatchWriteItem batching.
